items.title: Tropical cyclones now release ocean carbon, but warming could flip role by 2035
items.summary: The ocean is an important carbon sink that absorbs 20–30% of the total anthropogenic CO2 emissions in the industrial era (1.0–3.0 Pg annually, 1 Pg = 1015 g). Tropical cyclones are among the most devastating weather systems that profoundly disturb the upper ocean. However, their role in the global c

items.title: The Southwest's drought is shrinking wildlife's suitable habitat, with predators hit hardest
items.summary: As people in the United States are coping with historic drought conditions, the country's wildlife is also facing problems because of the extreme aridity. Herbivores, omnivores, and carnivores in the southwestern U.S. have all seen the extent of their suitable habitat shrink due to drought, acc

items.title: China launches crewed space flight as part of moon ambitions
items.summary: China launched its crewed Shenzhou-23 spacecraft and eased it into a successful docking with a space station early Monday as part of Beijing's ambitions to send humans to the moon by 2030, state media said.

items.title: With ice cream and giant fans, hajj pilgrims battle searing heat
items.summary: To avoid the punishing sun, Inas Gamal abandoned her ambitious plan of spending the days ahead of the hajj praying in Mecca's Grand Mosque and retreated to the comfort of an air-conditioned hotel room to perform her daytime prayers.

items.title: Prva astronautkinja iz Hong Konga poletjela u svemir
items.summary: PRVA astronautkinja iz Hong Konga, Li Jiaying, poletjela je u svemir u sklopu kineske misije Shenzhou-23. Tročlana posada boravit će na svemirskoj postaji Tiangong gdje će provoditi eksperimente.

items.title: Otkrivena sićušna hobotnica plave boje na Galapagosu
items.summary: ISTRAŽIVAČI su otkrili novu vrstu hobotnice u dubokom moru Galapagoskog otočja, prema nedavno objavljenom radu.

items.title: Adorable tiny blue octopus found nearly 6,000 feet beneath the Galápagos
items.summary: A mysterious little blue octopus discovered nearly 6,000 feet beneath the waters of the Galápagos Islands has officially been identified as a brand-new species. About the size of a golf ball, the tiny creature stunned researchers during a deep-sea expedition when it suddenly appeared on camera, craw

items.title: Beet juice lowers blood pressure in older adults in just 2 weeks
items.summary: Drinking nitrate-rich beetroot juice may do more than support heart health — it could actually reshape the bacteria living in the mouth in ways that help lower blood pressure in older adults. In the largest study of its kind, researchers found that older people who drank concentrated beetroot juice

items.title: Šećer može trajno naštetiti pamćenju, otkriva nova analiza
items.summary: NOVA analiza studija na glodavcima pokazuje da prehrana bogata šećerom trajno šteti pamćenju. Iako prelazak na zdravu prehranu pomaže, oporavak nije potpun, sugerirajući da je dio štete nepovratan.

items.title: Scientists supercharge natural killer cells to fight aggressive cancers
items.summary: Scientists at McGill University have found a way to supercharge the immune system’s natural killer (NK) cells, helping them break through the defenses tumors use to stay alive. By temporarily blocking two proteins, researchers turned these cells into far more effective cancer fighters against diffic

items.title: Scientists discover hidden liver switch that cuts harmful cholesterol
items.summary: Scientists at UT Southwestern have uncovered a surprising new “master switch” that helps control how much cholesterol the liver sends into the bloodstream. The newly identified protein, HELZ2, works by shutting down the genetic instructions needed to produce apoB — a key building block of the choles

items.title: Expedition to Hess Rise in the Northwest Pacific begins
items.summary: Located in the middle of the North Pacific, between Japan and Canada, lies one of the world's largest oceanic plateaus, the so-called Hess Rise. The plateau is roughly T-shaped and extends over a length of about 1,000 kilometers. Due to its distance from the nearest mainland, the research area

items.title: This tiny blue octopus from the Galápagos could curl up in your hand and shows how much deep ocean remains unexplored
items.summary: The Galápagos Islands off the coast of Ecuador are home to more than a thousand plant and animal species found nowhere else on Earth—things like marine iguanas and giant tortoises. In a new paper in the journal Zootaxa, scientists have announced the discovery of the newest animal found in the Galápa

items.title: The Pennine hills are full of holes—here's how they're helping fight climate change
items.summary: Thousands of holes are appearing in the Pennine hills, as part of efforts to improve carbon storage by restoring damaged peatland.

items.title: Cooling poverty is making extreme heat more dangerous for millions
items.summary: Imagine walking along Ipanema beach on a summer afternoon. The sand is golden, there's a cooling sea breeze, the shade of a parasol and a cold drink in hand. Now look up.

items.title: Graphene quantum dots show promise in targeting Parkinson's-related protein clumping
items.summary: The buildup of a protein called 𝛂-synuclein (ASN) into toxic clumps is a hallmark of synucleinopathies, a group of neurodegenerative diseases that includes Parkinson's and multiple system atrophy (MSA). These aggregates are associated with cellular dysfunction and lead to progressive neuronal

items.title: Fear silences women politicians in public debate, surveys suggest
items.summary: Women politicians are significantly more likely than their male colleagues to refrain from speaking out in public for fear of threats or harassment—particularly on issues relating to migration and gender equality. Politicians with an immigrant background are also more likely to avoid making public s

items.title: When order gives way to chaos—the turbulent birth of magnetic nanovortices
items.summary: Magnetic switching processes are considered a prime example of controllable physics at the nanometer scale: in certain thin-film systems, a short electrical current pulse is sufficient to reverse the magnetization in a targeted way. items.title: Companies struggle to realize circular ambitions, says researcher
items.summary: The research, conducted by Wierikx at the Department of Industrial Engineering & Innovation Sciences, is based on more than 3,000 organizational assessments. The results reveal a consistent pattern: companies are relatively strong in setting circular ambitions, but face major challenges in scaling i

items.title: Očinstvo dramatično mijenja mozak muškarca, otkriva studija
items.summary: NOVO istraživanje otkriva da i očevi prolaze kroz značajne promjene na mozgu nakon rođenja djeteta. Mozak se restrukturira kako bi se prilagodio novim zahtjevima i ojačao roditeljske vještine.
